guidance.promptSnippet: Track multi-step work when a visible task list will genuinely help.
promptGuidelines: Use todo for substantial multi-step work where a visible task list helps track progress: several user-requested outcomes, explicit dependencies, a long-running implementation/debugging session, or work likely to span many tool calls.

Prefer doing the work directly without todo for short, linear tasks: simple edits, quick searches, one-command checks, small explanations, code review comments, or early exploration before concrete follow-up work is known.
Do not create a todo list just to mirror a generic workflow. Avoid filler items like investigate, implement, test unless each item maps to a distinct user-visible outcome.
Do not create a one-item todo list. If only one useful trackable item exists, handle it directly and use brief progress updates in chat instead.
When todo is useful, keep the list short and outcome-oriented. Each task should represent meaningful progress the user would care about, not internal bookkeeping.
Keep exactly one item in_progress while working. Mark items completed as soon as they are fully done, and never mark incomplete or blocked work as completed.
When all requested work is complete, mark remaining items completed and clear the list if the tool supports clearing, so no completed-only todo UI remains visible.
